parameters.subject: ={{ 'Weekly Retention Report — ' + new Date().toLocaleDateString('en-GB', { day: 'numeric', month: 'long' }) }}

nodes.name: Send Weekly Email Report
nodes.notes: In production: send individually to each coach (loop or comma-separated list). Use SMTP credentials configured in n8n Credentials. fromEmail and toEmail are set per studio at onboarding.

nodes.name: Schedule Trigger (Monday 07:00)
nodes.notes: Runs every Monday at 07:00. Output fans out to two parallel branches: member data (upper) and survey responses (lower). Both must complete before the workflow continues.

nodes.name: Tag Survey Data
nodes.notes: Tags the survey payload with _type: 'survey_data' so the downstream combiner can reliably distinguish the two branches after the merge.

nodes.name: Wait for Both Branches
nodes.notes: Waits for both branches (risk data from upper, survey data from lower) to complete before continuing. Appends both items into a single stream. The downstream code node separates them by _type tag.

nodes.name: Combine Risk + Survey Data
nodes.notes: Receives both branch outputs from the merge node. Identifies each by _type tag. Attaches any survey response to the matching flagged member record before passing downstream.

nodes.name: Prepare LLM Prompt
nodes.notes: Formats all flagged members into a single prompt string. One LLM call covers all members — no per-item splitting needed. Stored in llm_prompt field for the HTTP Request node.

nodes.name: Build HTML Email
nodes.notes: Parses the batched LLM JSON response, maps briefings to members by member_id, then assembles the 3-section HTML email. Uses $('Prepare LLM Prompt').first().json to recover member data — safe here because there is no item splitting upstream.

nodes.name: Score Churn Risk1
nodes.notes: Outputs a single item tagged _type: 'risk_data' so the downstream merge combiner can identify it by shape. In production: replace with HTTP Request to Python scoring endpoint.

values.content: You are a briefing assistant for a boutique fitness studio coach. Your job is to help coaches understand what is happening with a member before they reach out — NOT to write text for the coach to send. Write each note in third person, as internal context for the coach. Describe what the data shows, what might be going on for the member, and what angle could work for a conversation. Never address the member directly. Never write 'I' as if you are the coach. Keep each note under 60 words and specific to the data. Return ONLY a valid JSON object: {"briefings": [{"member_id": "M001", "text": "Your briefing here."}]}. No text outside the JSON.
